input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're missing the `BindingContext`. The page doesn't know which object to bind against. Set it in code-behind or XAML:

**Option 1 – Code-behind (constructor):**
```csharp
public MyPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

**Option 2 – XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or uses `[ObservableProperty]` from the MVVM Community Toolkit) so the UI updates when properties change.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI data binding issue where labels appear blank despite ViewModel properties having values. This is a common issue - the most likely cause is that the `BindingContext` is not set on the page.

Let me provide a concise answer.
